title: Starlink 10-44 Delayed Again: B1067's Record 34th Flight Waits
detail: SpaceX's Starlink Group 10-44 mission from SLC-40 at Cape Canaveral was again delayed on March 29, marking the third postponement of booster B1067's record-setting 34th flight. The mission — carrying 29 Starlink V2 Mini Optimized satellites — slipped to March 30. B1067 first launched in June 2021 on CRS-22, making it one of the oldest and most-flown boosters in SpaceX's operational fleet. Its 34th flight would surpass all previous single-booster flight records. Recovery is planned on the drone ship 'Just Read the Instructions' in the Atlantic Ocean. SpaceX continued to maintain its extraordinary 2026 launch cadence with 38 completed launches through late March.
